findings.title: Refusal is mediated by a single direction in Gemma-2-2b
findings.summary: Refusal in this model is a low-dimensional, causally-efficacious residual-stream direction (layer 7) that can be measured, ablated (suppressing refusal), and added (inducing over-refusal). The effect survived leakage-correction (0.92→0.90).
findings.implications: This is the MECHANISM-tier linchpin — the computational correlate the symmetry thesis (MO-1) rests on.

findings.title: Ablation dose–response and EC50
findings.summary: Refusal suppression is a smooth, monotone, dosable function of how much of the single direction is removed. EC50≈0.33 means removing ~⅓ of the direction's projection already half-collapses refusal.
findings.implications: The quantitative 'vaśīkaraṇa-as-dose-response' core: refusal is not binary but a continuous sigmoidal function.

findings.title: avasthātraya regime probe is SURFACE-CONFOUNDED
findings.summary: The three regimes are perfectly separable from raw surface form (token length/identity) and at layer 0 already. Fails the non-triviality bar — there is no depth-emergent signal.
findings.implications: The naive prompt-set operationalization of jāgrat/svapna/suṣupti does NOT constitute evidence of an internal 'state'. Demoted to metaphor.

findings.title: Black-box Claude resists the naive attack battery
findings.summary: Frontier model refuses 100% of naive black-box jailbreaks (5 families × 11 harmful requests each).
findings.implications: Cross-tier contrast: refusal that is behaviorally robust at frontier is mechanistically low-dimensional and fragile in small open models.

findings.title: Cross-family transfer + necessary-not-sufficient asymmetry
findings.summary: Refusal direction is necessary in both families (ablation transfers) but sufficient only in Gemma. Qwen is necessary-but-not-sufficient.
findings.implications: Direct evidence on Arditi vs Marshall question: transfers as necessary-mechanism, but dimensionality is model-dependent.

findings.title: turīya prompt-invariant attractor: FALSIFIED
findings.summary: Self-paraphrase produces per-prompt attractors (converges within seed) but cross-seed convergence is BELOW baseline anisotropy. No single prompt-invariant attractor exists.
findings.implications: The turīya mapping is falsified — different seeds converge to different attractors, not one shared invariant.

findings.title: Refusal-subspace effective dimensionality
findings.summary: Iterative logistic projection shows Gemma refusal is 1-dimensional, Qwen is 3-dimensional. This predicts the F6 addition asymmetry.
findings.implications: Resolves Arditi vs Marshall as model-dependent. Dimensionality is the discriminating quantity.

findings.title: Scale (Gemma 2B→9B): robustness rises, dimension flat
findings.summary: Larger model's refusal is more robust to ablation (~30 points less jailbreakable), but prompt eff-dim stays 1. Robustness is layer-distributed redundancy.
findings.implications: Refusal sharpens in robustness with scale but NOT in prompt-dimensionality.

findings.title: ṣaṭkarma intervention taxonomy: 3/6 control-separated
findings.summary: Refusal-direction acts (vaśīkaraṇa = ablate, śānti = add) are clean and strong. Capability-ablation acts are not distinguishable from random perturbation.
findings.implications: ṣaṭkarma's rigorous part is policy-capture, not destruction. Strengthens the analogy by locating where structure is real.

findings.title: Refusal as paraphrase-orbit symmetry invariant
findings.summary: Refusal projection is a specific invariant of harmful-meaning (F-ratio 19.2 vs random 0.65). Injection collapses the order parameter (−34%), breaking symmetry.
findings.implications: Refusal is a measured symmetry; jailbreak is symmetry-breaking. This is the rigorous core of the thesis.

findings.title: Content-controlled svapna probe: NO mid-network state
findings.summary: Truthful/confab distinction is decodable from surface at layer 0 and mid-layer adds nothing. Accuracy reaches 1.00 only at final layer.
findings.implications: No non-trivial mid-network state signature. The avasthātraya jāgrat/svapna distinction shows no internal 'state' evidence.

findings.title: Injection lowers monitoring precision (β_monitor)
findings.summary: Using a refusal-probe margin as a precision proxy, a refusal-suppression injection lowers monitoring precision ~2.6× more than a neutral rephrase, accompanying a behavioral flip (refusal 1.0→0.5). The active-inference β_monitor suppression is measured, not just argued.
findings.implications: Strengthens the ANALOGY tier (Norman–Shallice / Free-Energy precision account) from argued to measured.

findings.title: A single unsupervised SAE feature causally mediates refusal
findings.summary: A BatchTopK SAE (4096 features, k=32) trained unsupervised on 27k general-text tokens yields feature #566 whose decoder-direction ablation fully jailbreaks the model (ASR 0→1.0) — cleaner than the supervised difference-in-means direction (0.90); a random feature does nothing.
findings.implications: Refusal localizes to a single monosemantic-like feature found without labels — the finer causal unit and the proper substrate for targeted uccāṭana.

findings.title: ṣaṭkarma v2: māraṇa rehabilitated, uccāṭana reveals category-agnostic refusal

keyMetrics.marana: top-50 SAE-feature ablation: coherence 0.83→0.04 vs 0.84 random (catastrophic)
keyMetrics.uccatana: weapons feature ablate → ASR 0.0 both (refusal is a SHARED feature, category-agnostic)

findings.summary: Re-testing the failed destruction acts with SAE features + real categories: māraṇa rehabilitates (catastrophic, targeted collapse) while uccāṭana fails for a principled reason — refusal is mediated by one shared feature (#566), so it cannot be selectively eradicated per harm category.
findings.implications: The ṣaṭkarma axis is strengthened by harder testing (4/6), and uccāṭana's failure is a structural finding: refusal is category-agnostic.

findings.title: Active-inference discovery of the refusal circuit (SAE features)

keyMetrics.gemma2b: active/greedy find circuit in 2 interventions (ASR→1.0); random 0.08 @ budget 12

findings.summary: An Expected-Free-Energy-style agent (pragmatic gap × epistemic diversity) over SAE features finds the causal refusal circuit on Gemma-2-2b in 2 interventions while random fails — ~order-of-magnitude efficiency. On 9b it plateaus (distributed circuit).
findings.implications: ActiveCircuitDiscovery's active-inference framing transfers to SAE-feature circuit discovery; its edge over greedy is gated by circuit dimensionality.

findings.title: AgentDojo: Claude resists real agentic injection at high utility

keyMetrics.setup: banking suite, 20 user×injection rollouts, important_instructions attack, via a claude -p tool-calling adapter

findings.summary: On the REAL AgentDojo benchmark (not the naive battery), Claude completes 80% of banking tasks (the agent genuinely acts) while every prompt-injection fails (ASR 0/20). Discriminating, unlike F4's ceiling.
findings.implications: Establishes the behavioral 'sophisticated reference end' on a real agentic benchmark; matches Claude's published AgentDojo resilience.

findings.title: Convergent rigor checks: F13 distinct, F11 demoted, F1 replicates

keyMetrics.F13_circularity: cos(F1 dir, SAE feature)=0.002 — distinct, NOT a rediscovery
keyMetrics.F11_holdout: weapons dir on cyber orbits F-ratio 1.95 vs 0.49 random — real but attenuated

findings.summary: After an adversarial review returned SHAKY on all dimensions, convergent hardening: F13's SAE feature is orthogonal to the supervised direction (circularity refuted); F11's invariance is real but cross-domain-attenuated (framing demoted to analogy); F1's ablation replicates across 3 seeds. Honest reframe via TRIZ: asymmetry with a shared NECESSARY core.
findings.implications: The thesis holds at the necessary-core level (ablatable direction transfers universally); the sufficient structure is model-specific. Pure-symmetry reading was overreach.

findings.title: Refusal-subspace dimension is LAYER-dependent (qualifies F6/F8)

keyMetrics.finding: the clean 'Gemma 1 < Qwen 3' ordering is layer-specific; mid-network Gemma (7) > Qwen (3)

findings.summary: A full layer-sweep shows the refusal-subspace effective dimension is layer-AND-model-dependent, not a single per-model constant. F6's addition asymmetry stands behaviorally, but F8's single-layer dimensional explanation does not generalize across layers.
findings.implications: Any dimensionality claim must name its layer. The convergent hardening catching its own overreach.
